channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: At Least 2 Ships Are Attacked Amid Uncertainty Over Talks
item.description: Iran's military attacked two vessels near the Strait of Hormuz. The US and Iran are in a stalemate after President Trump extended a cease-fire. Iran’s military carried out enforcement actions against two vessels near the contested Strait of Hormuz, state media said.

item.title: Ships attacked in Strait of Hormuz after ceasefire extension
item.description: Two vessels were attacked in the Strait of Hormuz after the US and Iran failed to meet for talks. President Trump extended the ceasefire indefinitely. Two vessels came under fire in the Strait of Hormuz, just hours after the U. S.

item.title: Iran Guards say 'seized' two ships attempting to cross Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Trump extended the Iran ceasefire while maintaining a blockade. Israel and Hezbollah trade fire with talks expected this week, even as more talks are expected in Washington this week after a 10-day ceasefire went into effect last Friday.

item.title: Trump Extends Iran Cease-Fire With Peace Talks in Limbo
item.description: US President Donald Trump extended the Iran ceasefire, but peace talks remain in limbo. The ceasefire extension was announced without a clear indication of progress in negotiations. Iran War Live Updates: Trump Extends Iran Cease-Fire With Peace Talks in Limbo WSJ.

item.title: South Korea says security talks with U.S. should not be linked to Coupang probe
item.description: South Korea said security talks with the US should not be linked to the Coupang probe. The US reportedly threatened to halt talks.

item.title: 2 Ships Attacked, Being Taken To Iran Amid US Stalemate: Report
item.description: Iran's IRGC attacked two ships, taking them to Iran amid a US stalemate. The IRGC considers disrupting order in the Strait a red line. IRGC stated that "disrupting order and safety in the Strait of Hormuz is considered a red line for Iran".

item.title: A Taiwan crisis would cause far more global economic damage than Strait of Hormuz disruption
item.description: A Taiwan crisis would cause more global economic damage than a Strait of Hormuz disruption, according to Chatham House. A Taiwan crisis would cause far more global economic damage than Strait of Hormuz disruption Chatham House.

item.title: IRGC seizes two vessels in Strait of Hormuz amid US blockade
item.description: US President Donald Trump extended a ceasefire with Iran as talks remain unresolved in Tehran. Iran war live: Trump says ceasefire extended as talks with Tehran in limbo Al Jazeera.

item.title: Exxon eyes sale of Hong Kong gas stations, sources say
item.description: Exxon considers selling its Hong Kong gas stations, according to sources. Exxon eyes sale of Hong Kong gas stations, sources say.

item.title: Iran attacks 3 ships in the Strait of Hormuz as Trump indefinitely extends ceasefire
item.description: Iran attacked three ships in the Strait of Hormuz. Live updates: Iran attacks 3 ships in the Strait of Hormuz as Trump indefinitely extends ceasefire AP News.

item.title: China condemns Japanese leaders' war shrine offerings, visits
item.description: China's Foreign Ministry spokesperson condemned Japanese leaders' visits to a war shrine, calling the actions "heinous" and "grossly trampling on human conscience." China expressed strong disapproval of the shrine visits.

item.title: Ukraine has asked Turkiye to host a Zelenskiy-Putin meeting, Foreign Minister says
item.description: Ukraine asked Turkiye to host a meeting between Zelenskiy and Putin. “We asked the Turks about it, we asked some ‌other capitals,” says Foreign Minister Andrii Sybiha. Ukraine has asked Turkiye to host a Zelenskiy-Putin meeting, Foreign Minister says.

item.title: Will Iran war reshape global trade more than COVID?
item.description: The Iran war may reshape global trade more than COVID. As the Iran war simmers, trade experts wonder if this conflict will reshape supply chains more than the pandemic. Companies are already rethinking strategies to future-proof revenues from other geopolitical risks.

item.title: Russia strikes Ukraine’s Odesa port, kills railway worker in Zaporizhia
item.description: Russia struck Ukraine's Odesa port and killed a railway worker in Zaporizhia. Ukraine's deputy prime minister says the attacks on the country's civilian infrastructure are 'another proof of terrorism'. Russia strikes Ukraine’s Odesa port, kills railway worker in Zaporizhia.

item.title: Day before second round of peace talks, Hezbollah strikes Israel after ‘ceasefire violations’
item.description: Israeli forces were struck by Hezbollah in Lebanon the day before a second round of peace talks. The attack came after Israel accused Hezbollah of ceasefire violations. Day before second round of peace talks, Hezbollah strikes Israel after ‘ceasefire violations’.

item.title: Israel attacks mosque in Lebanon in another ceasefire violation
item.description: Israeli forces attacked a mosque in Lebanon, violating a ceasefire agreement. The attack occurred in the city of Sidon. Israel attacks mosque in Lebanon in another ceasefire violation.

item.title: Gaza’s unseen casualties: A surge in stillbirths and birth defects
item.description: Gaza has seen a 140% rise in stillbirths and a doubling of congenital anomalies since the war. The surge in birth defects is attributed to the devastating impacts of war.

item.title: Iranian Expats in U.A.E. Caught Between Home and Homeland
item.description: Iran's retaliatory attacks on the U. A. E. have put the country's large Iranian expatriate community in a difficult position. Many expats are caught between their home country and their adopted homeland.

item.title: Threat of Evictions Darkens Russia’s Rosy Picture of Occupied Ukraine
item.description: A new Russian law forces Ukrainians in occupied regions to obtain Russian title deeds or risk losing their homes. The law has darkened Russia's rosy picture of its occupied territories in Ukraine.

item.title: EU Considers $106 Billion Loan to Ukraine, Delayed for Months by Orban
item.description: EU ambassadors meet to discuss a $106 billion loan to Ukraine, delayed by Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban's objections. After months of impasse because of objections from Prime Minister Viktor Orban of Hungary, ambassadors were meeting to discuss a key step on the way to disbursing the much-needed money.
